author: [Peace DAC] TGI LizardMUD
content: Fleet Rentals will roll out very much as an MVP soft launch. It may or may not serve it's full purpose before we get to C4 and Council Rank. I'm curious to see what transpires on both the demand and supply side of this market, as well as seeing price discovery play out. However, dovetailing on a few comments shared in fr-chat ( @[AEP] Funcracker), I do believe there will be a few sources of supply side liquidity, including:  Faction Fleet users transitioning assets to Fleet Rentals (if better income exists there),Existing players with un/under-utilized assets enlisting fleets. I personally fall in this camp, with a number of smaller ships that I just don't bother forming fleets with -- I primarily used those ships for the crew, but now have the ability to transport those crew around, so I don't really need the ships sitting idle,New entrants entering Star Atlas specifically because they see an opportunity to generate income by participating in fleet rentals, I personally have larger "concerns" with initial demand for rentals. We will need to see some player growth, coupled with CR restrictions, before rental markets really start to flourish imo. But between the features we have coming out leading up to C4 launch, and then of course the potential market increase in demand with C4 itself, I think we could see an explosion in rental activity around mid-year.  Separately, as Funcracker noted, we do have an additional proposal that could see ATMTA seeding some initial fleet liquidity into rental markets during the early stages of release.

content: If you search “competitive shooter controller aim assist” I think you will find the opposite and see it’s typical industry standard to provide this feature when users can use both mouse and keyboard and controllers on the same platform. Otherwise, mouse and keyboard has a significant advantage which alienates controller players. Not saying our controller aim assist feature can’t be tuned a bit, it likely can and this is good feedback, but to say no competitive games offer the feature is false.   I’ll also say, our near term goals aren’t to become the next esports shooter. It’s to lay the foundations for fun gunplay and also offer progression-enabled repeatable game modes to keep our players engaged while we build the longer-term mmo vision.

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: It's interesting hearing both sides of this, because there is a cohort that is concerned about the asset inflation in SAGE when we shut down Faction Fleet. Ultimately, FF contributes little to the economy at the moment, but serves as a decent retention mechanism because of the passive earnings potential. I think @Chri.z reported to me that FF produces less than 5% of the R4 in the economy today? I might be misquoting that. I would have been fine killing it a year ago, but we did want a smoother transition into a passive earnings module, which Fleet Rentals effectively brings online. I mentioned this before, but we're not immediately killing FF when FR launches, as we want to see how both liquidity and platform performance look before making that call. But the time for deprecation could be near. And we certainly do not need to move forward with the Locker benefits.

author: ROME | Winston [ROME]
content:
I am coming in after the battle here, but isn't it unfair to be able to generate the same rate of ATLAS with ships that have not been paid for ? (Referring to some of the ATMTA unsold supply going for rent)

If someone spent USD to purchase a ship, then rents this ship, there is no dilution we still have the same amount of ships active in the economy the rewards are simply split between two people.

If an unsold ship is rented, resources will be consumed and fees will be paid, but for it to be profitable there needs to be more ATLAS extracted from the reward pool. 

How does this not create a death spiral of extraction plunging the value of ATLAS and assets into oblivion ?

Someone renting who did not spend anything on ships has no incentive to reinvest the capital to purchase assets, he will just rent more and never shoulder the risk.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Yes, it will automatically be relisted with the existing state of the fleet (wherever it exists at the time of relisting). That said, the re-listing logic is configurable. So an owner can decide to automatically relist or not. Owners might find it preferable to manually relist so they can proactively manage fleet location and resources supplied so their fleets are more desirable. But that would require significantly more oversight and monitoring., Correct. We will be adding a separate security deposit function, which could be applied to the cost of resources, and account for location of fleet. So, much like a rental car, if you don't return it full of fuel, you'll be charged out of your deposit. And likewise, if it isn't at a SB, the renter will be charged for that. But those won't be in the MVP., This is an interesting one. Because crew state is based on a point value system, and not "physicalized" transport of actual cNFTs in-game right now, crew will be force-returned to the renter at the close of a rental, regardless of location of said fleet. I'm double checking where those units are returned. Whether that be the CSS, or the SB they were at when they were added to the fleet. For clarity, however, the fleet will have to be at the location of the units to add them to it in the first place. These units are treated separately from the crew added by the owner, which the renter can't interact with (can't disembark)., I'll note, the dashboard does display a fair amount of information about the state of a rental. author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: The owner must form fleets to rent them, which means they need to have crew onboard. They should only rent with the minimum crew required to operate. Having additional crew provides no benefit, and limits the renter from being able to add their own crew. The renter cannot interact with any of the crew on the Owner's fleet. In other words, those crew are always returned with the fleet.

author: Icy
content: Apollo functionality would be identical to Solana, as it is a direct clone of Solana. Not only do I think combatting automation would be nearly impossible, I also think that approach would be antithetical to an open access, permissionless, decentralized Cyber Nation. Keep in mind that you are interacting with an early stage, early access pre-alpha product. With more mechanics, deeper economy, more specialization, and guardrails like Council Rank, I think you'll see some more of that balance come to fruition. But right now, early adopters get more of a free for all. And separately, we actually want to increase automation and automation accessibility, particularly as we release more UE product features. Let people focus more on the fun, and less on managing productive operations.

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: I've been playing it a bit, so I can hop in high level. New players start with a seamless onboarding experience by simply "starting" the game. A wallet is created in the background (locally), and a few assets are airdropped on the player, consisting of some crew, a few ships, and some ATLAS (on "Apollo"). The player then progresses through a basic mission system, which largely teaches people how to play the game. Form a fleet. Load in some inventory (R4). Mine some resources. Travel to various starbases. Craft various items... The primary objective of the game is to increase the size of operations via ship crafting. And the mission system walks people through the process of accomplishing this. Currently, the final mission is to craft a Titan. Everything in the game moves at (probably) 10x speed.   Different missions and game winning objectives can come from this in the future. But I have to say, it's an incredible onboarding experience for new users, and I could absolutely see it converting players to mainnet.

author: GoldenLover | BubblePop
content:
Has the team considered lowering marketplace fees for MRZ? Right now, most local marketplace trades happen OTC. Players list at the minimum price, then handle the real transaction privately to avoid fees. This means the marketplace barely collects any revenue, and liquidity suffers.

I know we are meant to lock ATLAS for lower fees but most players don't have the ability to stack enough ATLAS for this and most of the fee is currently the margin on most items. It's very difficult to provide liquidity to MRZ nodes as a buyer when you never expect to absorb any of the selling due to side stepping the current fee system being optimal. Also some of us want volumes where we would need to do 50+ OTC trades a day if we want to compete.

Lower fees would improve market efficiency. More listings and bids lead to better price discovery, faster trades, and a healthier economy. Plus, it brings in smaller traders who currently avoid the system altogether.

Right now, high fees push transactions out of the system. Lowering them brings those trades back in, boosting total revenue while making the marketplace better for everyone.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Allowing guilds to ban specific players from bases could lead to power abuse, excessive gatekeeping, and a fragmented player economy. It may enable dominant guilds to monopolize key trade hubs, limiting interaction and shutting out competitors. This could encourage player griefing, undermine existing tax and tariff systems, and lead to workarounds like alt-account abuse (which has not been solved yet).  Proposal  Create system of tiered access implementing restricted services based on reputation, standing, or faction ties (e.g., outsiders can only use basic markets, while guild members get full benefits.),Let sanctions have higher fees rather than bans.,Permit trade negotiations which will allow trade embargoes to expire after certain conditions are met (rather than ban)., Thank you for your time.

This could encourage player griefing, undermine existing tax and tariff systems, and lead to workarounds like alt-account abuse (which has not been solved yet). author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: I think you guys have kind of answered a lot of your own questions in the FR Chat discussions today. However, just some comments from me:  I don't think lowering fees in MRZ will result in better liquidity or activity on those local markets. Any fee is competing against OTC at 0%. People are still going to go for 0% for as long as they can. The solution to this is to plug the "exploit" of being able to do OTC at all. There's a few ways we could accomplish this. The most direct of which is to force best execution order books and remove the direct order matching auction house style for commodity markets. We originally moved towards auction house to prepare for individualized/customized 1:1 game assets, but it doesn't really make sense for commodities. Team is fully tasked working on C4 and UE updates, though, so marketplace updates won't come until the latter half of this year at the earliest., On "freighting" career mode -- unfortunately the game really isn't designed for this at the moment. And any short term solution for this is just a distraction from core development. Again, fully focused on C4 features in SAGE. Eventually we will have the Job Board that will let people take these contracts without having to purchase the items before selling at the destination starbase. We just aren't there yet., Regarding Solana marketing -- in this particular instance, they are including other projects in content, but made a similar post/RT with us in it earlier in the week. Ironically, @DominicVain came up with the "Pivot to real" narrative that they are now running with. Otherwise, I'd certainly like to see greater marketing support from Solana. But they have dedicated very little time to gaming in general. We do have channels open with them, but we can't tell them how to run their own strategy.,

Team is fully tasked working on C4 and UE updates, though, so marketplace updates won't come until the latter half of this year at the earliest. , On "freighting" career mode -- unfortunately the game really isn't designed for this at the moment. And any short term solution for this is just a distraction from core development. Again, fully focused on C4 features in SAGE. Eventually we will have the Job Board that will let people take these contracts without having to purchase the items before selling at the destination starbase. We just aren't there yet. , Regarding Solana marketing -- in this particular instance, they are including other projects in content, but made a similar post/RT with us in it earlier in the week. Ironically, @DominicVain came up with the "Pivot to real" narrative that they are now running with. Otherwise, I'd certainly like to see greater marketing support from Solana. But they have dedicated very little time to gaming in general. We do have channels open with them, but we can't tell them how to run their own strategy. ,

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content:
I've spoken with Ben(head of staking at Solan Foundation) and mentioned Apollo to him. He immediately wanted to introduce me/SA team to Abk Labs which focuses on building L2s/SVMs on Solana.  Turns out SA team has already met Abk Labs so the introduction was slightly unnecessary.  What I took from these conversations is that ATMTA is already at the forefront of this technology and could be very well be a pioneer (again) in this technology. I had to read between the lines on this very very limited conversation so take this with a grain of Salt.

re: the Solana Foundation. I'll give a few comments here. It is a very very small team.  I've heard Dan Albert speak many times about their vision for the Foundation and it basically sums up to a proud parent boosting their kid out at 18 to make their way into the world.  They want to setup teams for success, but do not want teams reliant on them.  They will of course help out if there is specific technology or a role they can play that only they can do, such as developing relationships and building up the dev scene.   If you see a lack of attention from the foundation it's 1) these guys are stretched incredibly thin that this sentence doesnt do it justice, e.g. no time. And 2) from their eyes star atlas has already made it and probably doesn't need their help.

And a few notes on "Solana.".  This is a huge organization with lots of employees, in fact sometimes I don't know where it starts and who is actually hiring people. Or what entity they are hiring for.  Two breakpoints ago I knew they hired a few gaming people folk to help steer Solana for gaming.  This was likely on the backend so discussions directly with dev teams etc.  things are moving but just know it's a big team with people with different opinions.

Just because a Solana tweet does not mention Star Atlas does not mean Toly hates us. He probably has no idea that the tweet went out.  It's probably like 100 steps removed.   If you want Tolys attention you need to post amazing content and he might repost it.  That's the best way to get his attention imo.

The Solana Gaming tag is huge bullish news.  Its a signal that Solana is paying attention to gaming. Or at least some part of their team is.  Let's fully support that and not get angry if we aren't showcased on every tweet.  If we are generous and support Solana + other gaming projects I firmly believe it will come back to us.  There are real people behind every Solana post. Let's motivate them that Star Atlas is the best community on Solana and that they have fun covering us. Love not hate. 

P.s. "pivot to real products." LOVE IT.
